args.query: SELECT "Index", SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days, SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days FROM index_trade WHERE "Date" >= '2018-01-01' AND "Date" < '2019-01-01' AND "Index" IN ('NYA','IXIC','GSPTSE') GROUP BY "Index";

exec_args.sql: SELECT "Index", SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" > "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS up_days, SUM(CASE WHEN "Close" < "Open" THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS down_days FROM index_trade WHERE "Date" >= '2018-01-01' AND "Date" < '2019-01-01' AND "Index" IN ('NYA','IXIC','GSPTSE') GROUP BY "Index";

args.code:
import json, pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame(var_call_0RMzr5bxmXJ3ARG8XvQvd67q)
# convert to numeric
for c in ['up_days','down_days']:
    df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c])
res = df[df['up_days']>df['down_days']].sort_values('Index')
answer = res.to_dict(orient='records')
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(answer))

result.preview: "Indices in North America with more up days than down days in 2018:\n- GSPTSE (Toronto Stock Exchange / S&P/TSX Composite): 44 up days vs 41 down days\n- IXIC (NASDAQ Composite): 44 up days vs 31 down days"
